review: AWS ECS runtime-readiness plan set — round 9 worktree-only orchestration repair
object: docs/superpowers/plans/aws AWS ECS plan/review set, orchestration run sheet, and runtime-readiness/universal-policy designs @ feat/aws-ecs-program
process: Independent orchestration-contract, mechanical, and Wardline re-review of the planning repair in the isolated program worktree. The review covered immutable PROGRAM_BASE_SHA ancestry, ignored concurrent documentation through Stages 1-8, substantive documentation classification, Stage 9 pre-merge inventory and conflict resume, Plan 10 replacement lineage, Plan 12 cleanup ordering, candidate-bound Major Checkpoint 6, exact final fast-forward, shell fences, links, JSON, whitespace, stale terms, and SHA256 bindings.
verdict_summary: GO to execute the dependency DAG in one ignored feat/aws-ecs-program worktree. Concurrent main/release documentation is ignored through Stages 1-8. Stage 9 reviews and merges the current release tip, records RECONCILED_RELEASE_SHA, and runs the reconciliation checkpoint before Task 1 freezes the candidate. Runtime GO still requires Major Checkpoint 6 and Plan 12 Tasks 1 through 8 on that unchanged SHA, cleanup, exact-SHA hosted CI, and the final fast-forward from the unchanged reconciliation anchor.

non_blocking_residuals.title: Runtime acceptance remains external
non_blocking_residuals.disposition: Plans 10/12 own real Aurora/ECS/task-role/Guardrail/telemetry execution and zero-skip proof; planning approval is not runtime GO.

non_blocking_residuals.title: Epoch 23 needs operator-controlled recreation for populated old Landscape databases
non_blocking_residuals.disposition: Archive/export, destructive reset, compatibility approval, and rollback decisions remain database/release-operator responsibilities.

non_blocking_residuals.title: AWS harmful-content coverage lacks Azure self_harm parity
non_blocking_residuals.disposition: The residual is explicit; equivalent coverage requires another approved control or an operator decision.

non_blocking_residuals.title: Hosted CI does not build the lean INSTALL_EXTRAS image
non_blocking_residuals.disposition: Plan 10 and Plan 12 perform the real lean build/run; retain the existing overview strengthening follow-up.

repairs_verified: Plan 15 was split into 15A text sink, 15B universal policy, and 15C thin Bedrock Guardrail implementation with separate tracker ownership and review artifacts.
The approved required core is CSV/JSON/text source and sink plus field_mapper, web_scrape, and operator-profiled LLM for the canonical tutorial; XML remains parked.
Plan 15A is byte-oriented, race-safe, multi-batch preserving, rollback-aware, and source-hash/golden/discovery complete.
Plan 15B binds policy to exact plugin code identities, resolves per-principal usable aliases without credential-scope shadowing, and keeps private lowering memory-only.

Plan 15B enforces the same snapshot at catalog, freeform, guided, recipes, import, validation, execution, delayed export, frontend cache, readiness, and audit boundaries while leaving CLI unrestricted.
Required prompt/content controls use typed input/output roles, fail-safe trust classification, and graph dominance/post-dominance; recommendation never auto-inserts topology.
Landscape epoch 23 has explicit SQLite and PostgreSQL old-populated refusal/recreate behavior, atomic policy evidence, audit-before-telemetry ordering, and release compatibility ownership.
Plan 15C recursively pins the installed ApplyGuardrail model, validates complete usage, blocks detect-only positives, accepts bounded 1..N intervention outputs, and discards provider text.
Full CLI catalog schemas remain explicit while web policy-view schemas expose only usable opaque aliases and safe options.
Plan 12 mechanically checks all 19 pre-closeout tracker steps: 18 application slices plus the verification-baseline task; the live milestone has 20 steps including closeout.
PROGRAM_BASE_SHA is the immutable Stages 1-8 ancestry marker; unrelated concurrent documentation is not inspected or gated until Stage 9.
Stage 9 inventories and classifies the release-only delta before merge, reconstructs conflict-resume anchors without resampling release, and preserves all historical close anchors.
Plan 10 itself and consumed prerequisites use the owner-approved replacement-lineage protocol when a substantive reconciliation delta invalidates the historical baseline.
The reconciliation affected-area checkpoint precedes Task 1; Major Checkpoint 6 runs only on the frozen candidate before Task 2, and external cleanup precedes any post-mutation rereconciliation.
verification: balanced Markdown fences across all four amended orchestration documents
all 75 Bash or shell fences in those documents pass bash -n
all 24 local links in those documents resolve
all AWS ECS review artifacts parse as JSON
targeted stale-term searches found no RELEASE_START_SHA or paused-release instruction in the amended documents
a synthetic divergent-release repository passed conflict-class exit, fresh-shell anchor reconstruction, staged resolution, reconciliation commit, and preserved ancestry
independent orchestration-contract and Wardline reviewers returned APPROVED after repair
every reviewed source SHA256 matches its refreshed sidecar or aggregate source_sha256 binding

review_history.summary: Telemetry and Guardrails expansion identified the need for AWS operational telemetry and explicit AWS-native prompt/content controls.

The universal-policy pass found text-sink rollback/race defects, under-bound per-alias and private-config policy evidence, incomplete cache/trust/schema controls, response-model guesses, and stale dependency/closeout metadata.
All blockers were repaired; final independent re-reviews returned GO for Plans 15A, 15B, and 15C.

All AWS plan/review artifacts moved into the aws folder; path-bearing repository and tracker references were repaired; an orchestration run sheet now pins the live 20-step DAG, integration-before-close protocol, stage barriers, rollback baseline, and Plan 12 handoff.

The single-worktree autonomous model was independently re-reviewed after repair. The release-ancestry stop condition, stale start-next/merge/rebase wording, full-suite timing, exact-SHA CI path, and review hash bindings now agree across the prompt, run sheet, Plan 00, Plan 10, and Plan 12.

The worktree-only Stages 1-8 contract and explicit Stage 9 release reconciliation were independently re-reviewed. Conflict resume, substantive documentation classification, Plan 10 replacement lineage, candidate-bound Major Checkpoint 6, cleanup-before-rereconciliation, and review hashes now agree.
